accepted.assertion:
Erdős Problem #572: declared status 'open'. Formalized: no. Show that for $k\geq 3$\[\mathrm{ex}(n;C_{2k})\gg n^{1+ rac{1}{k}}.\] Current best: It is easy to see that $\mathrm{ex}(n;C_{2k+1})=\lfloor n^2/4floor$ for any $k\geq 1$ (and $n>2k+1$) (since no bipartite graph contains an odd cycle). Lazebnik, Ustimenko, and Woldar \cite{LUW95} have shown that, for arbitrary $k\geq 3$,\[\mathrm{ex}(n;C_{2k})\gg n^{1+ rac{2}{3k-3+
u}},\]where $
u=0$ if $k$ is odd and $
u=1$ if $k$ is even. Prize: no. Tags: graph theory, turan number.
